result:
No header file includes `eloop.h` at all, confirmed with two independent greps (quoted-include search and a broader `eloop\.h` + include search across all `.h` files — both empty).

## Transitive closure check

I searched every `.h` file in the tree for `#include "eloop.h"` — **zero headers include it**. Since no header re-exports `eloop.h`, there's no indirect path: a `.c` file can only see `eloop.h`'s declarations by including it directly. So the transitive closure (via any header that includes `eloop.h`) is the **empty set**, and trivially matches — no additional `.c` files gain `eloop.h` visibility through a header; the direct-include count of **59** is the complete and exact set.

(Note: I used grep for this rather than the LSP tool, since "which files contain this #include directive" is a text-search question, not a symbol-navigation one — clangd's index doesn't expose a reverse-`#include` query. I did double-check with two independent grep patterns to rule out a missed case.)
